Description: Higher empathy predicts lower moral-judgment scores for harmful decisions.
Interpretation: H1 is inconclusive: fail to reject the null. The coefficient direction is negative but the estimate is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

Description: Same-faculty harm receives lower moral-judgment scores than cross-faculty harm.
Interpretation: H2a is inconclusive: fail to reject the null. The coefficient direction is negative but the estimate is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

Description: Outgroup perpetrators receive lower moral-judgment scores than ingroup perpetrators.
Interpretation: H2b is inconclusive: fail to reject the null. The coefficient direction is positive but the estimate is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

Description: Empathy becomes more negative in outgroup than ingroup cases.
Interpretation: H3 is inconclusive: fail to reject the null. The coefficient direction is negative but the estimate is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
